article_items.title: Anthropic blocks all customers' access to Fable 5 and Mythos 5 - Engadget
article_items.description: Anthropic has suspended all access to its new AI models Fable 5 and Mythos 5 to comply with a government order citing national security concerns.
article_items.excerpt: Anthropic has suspended all access to its new AI models Fable 5 and Mythos 5 to comply with a government order citing national security concerns.
article_items.summary: The company has just launched the Fable AI model, which was designed to bring many of Mythos' capabilities to the public, on June 9. The company said in its announcement that the US government wanted it to suspend all foreign nationals' access to its newly launched AI models, whether they're inside or outside the US and even if they're Anthropic employees, citing national security concerns. The government apparently gave the company verbal evidence for one potential narrow, non-universal jailbreak that an unnamed entity shared with officials. It beat Pokémon FireRed during the company's tests, for instance, while Claude failed to beat Pokémon Red , the original game it was based on. Anthropic promised to share more details over the next 24 hours, but it clarified that it disagrees that a potential jailbreak should be cause for recalling a commercial model. Anthropic has suspended all access to its new AI models Fable 5 and Mythos 5 to comply with a government order citing national security.

Anthropic has suspended all access to its new AI models Fable 5 and Mythos 5 to comply with a government order citing national security concerns.
Fable's capabilities "exceed" any previous model Anthropic has launched.
While the US government didn't specify those concerns, Anthropic believes that it's because the government heard about a method of jailbreaking Fable 5.
If you'll recall, Mythos is its state-of-the-art cybersecurity model that's only available to its Project Glasswing partners.
All its other models and its Claude chatbot are not affected.
Anthroic has disabled all of its customers' access to Fable 5 and Mythos 5 in order to ensure compliance with an order it received from the government on Friday, June 12.
Anthropic promised to share more details over the next 24 hours, but it clarified that it disagrees that a potential jailbreak should be cause for recalling a commercial model.

article_items.title: OpenAI is facing investigation from a group of state attorneys general - Engadget
article_items.description: A coalition of state attorneys general is asking OpenAI for documents about its activities.
article_items.excerpt: A coalition of state attorneys general is asking OpenAI for documents about its activities.
article_items.summary: "We take the concerns raised by state attorneys general seriously and intend to engage constructively with their offices." It's unclear what prompted the investigation, but tech companies developing AI products have been under scrutiny by state AGs for quite a while now. On Friday, June 12, the company received a subpoena seeking information and documents related to its activities and impact on users. They also want to know about the company's activities related to minor and senior users, its deep learning models, its policies and its models' sycophancy. Based on what the publication saw, the AGs are asking for documentation about the company's advertising, user engagement and retention, as well as its handling of its users' data and health information. It hasn't decided on timing and pricing yet. The lawsuit claimed that the plaintiff's daughter who died by suicide discussed her suicidal thoughts and plans with the chatbot in the months leading up to her death.

Last year, a group of 44 state AGs sent a letter to Meta, Google, Apple, Microsoft, OpenAI, Anthropic, Perplexity AI and XAI, asking them to protect children from being exposed to inappropriate and potentially harmful.

Based on what the publication saw, the AGs are asking for documentation about the company's advertising, user engagement and retention, as well as its handling of its users' data and health information.

In April, Florida Attorney General James Ulthmeier opened a criminal investigation into OpenAI, because the suspect in the 2025 Florida State University mass shooting reportedly used ChatGPT.
However, the company didn't alert the family or authorities.
The Journal said it viewed the subpoena sent by New York's attorney general.
OpenAI was named as a defendant in the first ever wrongful death lawsuit linked to a chatbot, as well.

article_items.title: Chinese cybercrime operation that used AI to scam 'hundreds of thousands of victims' sued by Google | TechCrunch
article_items.description: The tech giant said a group called "Outsider Enterprise" used AI to scam hundreds of thousands of victims, sending 2.5 million text messages over a span of two weeks.
